name: Linares de la Sierra - Los Marines – circular by Pico La Era
name_original: Linares de la Sierra - Los Marines – circular por Pico La Era
region_intro: Hiking around Linares De La Sierra offers trails through a deep, wooded valley, approximately 500 meters above sea level, within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The landscape is characterized by extensive dehesas, featuring evergreen oak, holm oak, acorn, and chestnut trees, alongside traditional agricultural areas with orchards. Numerous streams, acequias, and springs, such as Arroyo Plamencia, contribute to the region's water culture and provide natural features along the routes. Best hiking trails around Linares De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Linares de la Sierra - Los Marines – circular by Pico La Era, a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Linares de la Sierra – Linares de la Sierra Square loop from Linares de la Sierra, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding dehesas and agricultural landscapes. Local hikers also love the Los Marines Village – Aracena town centre loop from Linares de la Sierra, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail leading through wooded valleys and traditional villages, often completed in about 5 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Linares De La Sierra is defined by wooded valleys, dehesas with oak and chestnut trees, and a network of streams and springs. The region offers routes for various ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging, longer trails. The routes in Linares De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Linares De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Linares de la Sierra?
A: There are over a dozen hiking trails around Linares de la Sierra, offering a variety of experiences within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community.

Q: What makes hiking in Linares de la Sierra unique?
A: Hiking here offers a unique blend of natural beauty and cultural charm. Trails wind through deep, wooded valleys, extensive dehesas with evergreen and holm oaks, and traditional agricultural areas. The region's rich 'water culture' is evident with numerous streams and springs, and you'll encounter the picturesque 'Andalusian White Village' of Linares de la Sierra itself, known for its cobbled streets and unique 'llanos' (decorated stone floors).

Q: Are there hiking trails for different skill levels in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Yes, the area offers trails for various abilities. While most routes are classified as moderate, there are also more challenging options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983720209/linares-de-la-sierra-los-marines-circular-by-pico-la-era" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares de la Sierra - Los Marines – circular by Pico La Era&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.8 km trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Marines Village – Aracena town centre loop from Linares de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a more difficult 20.6 km route.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Linares de la Sierra. You can explore trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983720209/linares-de-la-sierra-los-marines-circular-by-pico-la-era" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares de la Sierra - Los Marines – circular by Pico La Era&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.8 km, or the 10.7 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares de la Sierra – Linares de la Sierra Square loop from Linares de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features along the hiking trails?
A: The region is known for its 'water culture.' You'll find numerous streams, acequias (Moorish irrigation channels), and springs like Fuente Nueva and Fuente Vieja. The Arroyo Plamencia is a notable stream that contributes to the area's natural features. There is also a specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7287170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; that can be visited.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: Beyond the stunning dehesas and wooded valleys, you can discover several natural landmarks. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arias Montano Peak&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views, and the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotto of the Marvels&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular cave system. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there charming villages or historical sites to explore near the trails?
A: Absolutely. Linares de la Sierra itself is a declared site of cultural interest, a quaint 'Andalusian White Village' with medieval charm, cobbled streets, and unique 'llanos.' Nearby, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4090741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Marines Village&lt;/a&gt; or explore Aracena, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aracena Castle&lt;/a&gt; with panoramic views.

Q: Is parking available for hikers in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Linares de la Sierra, particularly within the village itself. Specific trailheads may have designated parking areas, but it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park?
A: Generally, day hiking in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park does not require a permit for established trails. However, for specific activities or larger groups, it's always recommended to check with local park authorities for any updated regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Linares de la Sierra?
A: The best time for hiking in Linares de la Sierra is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is lush. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during that season. Winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Linares de la Sierra suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Linares de la Sierra are suitable for families, especially those looking for moderate walks. The varied terrain and natural features like streams and charming villages can make for an engaging experience. For a shorter, less strenuous option, consider routes that explore the immediate surroundings of the village.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Dogs are generally permitted on hiking trails within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Linares de la Sierra?
A: The hiking routes in Linares de la Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene natural beauty, the well-preserved traditional villages, and the diverse landscapes ranging from dehesas to wooded valleys and water features.
tips: Pico La Era (Geodesic Summit) Beautiful views of Linares de la Sierra.

The name of Los Marines has a curious history: during a visit by King Felipe II to the municipality, an inhabitant asked him why, being so powerful, he did not bring the sea to the town. The king's response was: "I will not bring the sea, but you will be sailors." Since then, all the young people of the town performed their military service in Marina Huelva.
This picturesque municipality, surrounded by nature and beauty, is an ideal place to explore during a getaway to the Sierra de Huelva. Don't hesitate to visit Los Marines and enjoy its charm.
